Organisation
System Name
Candidate Intake Form (Google Form / Typeform / Internal)
column3: Candidate name, phone, email, current city, desired role, current salary, work experience summary, consent record and timestamp
column5: Candidate intake, initial screening, consent capture
column7: Submitted directly by candidate via intake form
column9: Google LLC (if using Google Forms); Typeform Inc. (if using Typeform)
column12: Google Workspace 2FA, form access restricted to owner
column13: 12 months from last contact if not placed; 3 years from placement end if placed
column14: Delete form responses from Google Forms or equivalent. Delete corresponding rows from candidate tracker.
column17: CRITICAL: This replaces WhatsApp-only intake. Must include a consent checkbox clearly stating purpose and who data will be shared with. If not yet implemented — implement immediately.

column1: Candidate Database / Tracker (Google Sheets)
column3: Candidate name, phone, email, current city, skills, experience level, placement status, client assigned, salary offered, date of joining, current stage in pipeline
column5: Candidate tracking, recruitment pipeline management, placement reporting
column7: Entered by recruitment team from intake form responses and interviews
column8: Recruitment team, Founder only (restrict to named individuals)
column12: Google Workspace 2FA, sharing restricted to named individuals only — review quarterly
column13: 12 months from last contact (not placed) / 3 years from placement end (placed)
column14: Manual deletion of rows. Remove ex-staff access immediately on offboarding. Audit shared access quarterly.
column17: HIGH RISK. This sheet typically has too many people with access. Check sharing settings today. Remove access for anyone not actively using it.

column1: Google Drive — Candidate Documents
column3: Scanned Aadhaar, PAN card, passport photo, address proof (utility bill / rental agreement), educational certificates, employment certificates, bank account details
column5: Statutory compliance (PF/ESI registration), client pre-employment checks, KYC
column7: Uploaded by recruitment team after receiving documents from candidates
column12: Google Workspace 2FA, folder-level access restrictions, link sharing DISABLED
column13: During engagement. Delete Aadhaar and PAN scans after completing statutory registration (typically 30–60 days).
column14: Manual deletion folder by folder. Audit Drive for documents older than retention period.
column17: HIGHEST RISK ROW. Government ID scans in cloud storage must have strict access controls. Disable public link sharing. Delete IDs promptly after use. Consider encrypted local storage for most sensitive documents.

column1: WhatsApp Business (Communication)
column3: Candidate name, phone, job opportunity messages, delivery/location sharing, informal document images sent by candidates (if not controlled)
column5: Communication, job opportunity sharing, status updates
column6: Consent (Section 6) — implied by candidate messaging first
column7: Candidates and clients initiate or respond
column14: Manual deletion of chats. No bulk deletion available.
column17: CRITICAL: Do not accept Aadhaar, PAN, or bank documents via WhatsApp. Direct candidates to intake form for documents. WhatsApp acceptable for communication only. If document images have already been received — delete them from WhatsApp and request re-submission via secure channel.

column1: Recruiter Personal Email
column3: Resumes received, documents forwarded, candidate communications on personal email address
column5: Informal communication — SHOULD NOT BE PRIMARY CHANNEL
column7: Candidates forwarding documents or applying informally
column12: Personal account security — NOT controlled by organisation
column17: CRITICAL RISK: Organisation has no control over personal email accounts. Data held here is not auditable, not deletable on request, and not secured. Stop this practice immediately. All candidate communication must use company email accounts. Ask recruiters to forward any candidate data to company accounts and delete from personal.

column1: Background Verification (BGV) Agency
column3: Candidate name, Aadhaar number, PAN number, employment history dates, educational institution names, address for verification
column5: Pre-employment background verification for client placement
column7: Shared by organisation with BGV agency after candidate consent
column9: AuthBridge / IDfy / SpringVerify / HireRight or equivalent
column12: BGV agency platform security — confirm with agency
column13: Duration of engagement. Request deletion from BGV agency after verification is complete and report delivered.
column14: Request deletion from BGV agency portal. Confirm in writing.
column17: DPA REQUIRED with BGV agency. Candidate must specifically consent to BGV and be told which agency is used. Do not proceed with BGV without written candidate consent. Issue DPA now if not in place.

column1: Client HR Contact Database (CRM or Spreadsheet)
column3: Client company HR contact name, work phone, work email, designation, company name, city
column5: Client relationship management, requirement intake, placement coordination
column6: Legitimate use (B2B contact) — monitor DPDP Rules updates
column7: Collected directly from client during business development or via business card
column9: Google LLC (if using Sheets); CRM vendor if applicable
column14: Manual deletion from CRM or sheet on client relationship ending.
column17: B2B contact data is generally lower risk. Still personal data if individually identifiable. Monitor DPDP Rules for any B2B exemptions.

column1: Payroll Processing (Software or Manual Bank Transfers)
column3: Placed candidate / employee name, bank account number, IFSC code, UAN (PF number), ESI number, monthly salary, CTC breakdown, TDS deductions
column5: Salary disbursement, statutory deductions (TDS, PF, ESI), payslip generation
column7: Collected from employee during document submission
column9: Payroll software vendor (Keka / RazorpayX / Zoho Payroll) or external accountant
column12: Payroll software encryption, 2FA (if using software) / email security (if using accountant)
column14: Cannot delete bank and salary records within statutory period. Mark for deletion after 8 years.
column17: Consider moving from manual bank transfers to payroll software — reduces exposure of bank details. If sharing bank details with external accountant via email, use password-protected files and confirm accountant DPA.

column1: Government Portals (EPFO / ESIC / Income Tax)
column3: Employee / worker name, Aadhaar (linked), PAN (linked), UAN, ESI IP number, employer PF registration number, contribution records
column5: Statutory registration and contribution compliance — PF and ESI
column7: Entered by compliance team during onboarding / from employee documents
column12: Government portal security — password protect portal login accounts
column13: As per statutory requirements — do not delete statutory records
column17: Low risk category — government regulated. Ensure portal login credentials are stored securely and not shared verbally. Use unique passwords.

column1: Client Candidate Submission (Email / Client Portal)
column3: Candidate name, resume, contact details, assessment notes shared with client for approval
column5: Client approval process before candidate placement
column6: Consent (Section 6) — candidate should be informed of client sharing in consent notice
column7: Prepared by recruitment team from candidate database
column9: Client company (external) — client becomes Data Controller on receipt
column12: Email TLS in transit / client portal security
column13: Delete from own systems after placement. Client data retention governed by client.
column14: Delete from sent folder and shared files after placement confirmation.
column17: Candidate must be informed that their data will be shared with the client company. Include this disclosure in candidate intake consent notice. Do not share more data than necessary for the specific role.

column1: Add more rows above this line
PRIORITY ACTIONS FOR STAFFING BUSINESSES
1. Replace WhatsApp document collection with a digital intake form that includes consent checkbox — most critical gap (Section 6)
2. Restrict Google Sheet and Google Drive access — audit sharing settings today
3. Stop using personal recruiter email accounts for candidate data
4. Issue DPA with background verification agency before next BGV run
5. Delete Aadhaar and PAN scans from Google Drive after statutory registration is complete
6. Set a 12-month deletion trigger for non-placed candidates
This is not legal advice. Consult a qualified legal professional for advice specific to your organisation.
